epics.title: Require explicit run targeting for supervisor commands

tasks.title: Remove current-run pointer dependency from shared state
acceptance_criteria: Supervisor runtime no longer reads or writes .ai/state/current-run.json.
Existing run state remains addressed through .ai/runs/<run_id> directories.
The repository no longer documents a global active-run pointer.
done_definition: shared-state exports only per-run filesystem helpers.

tasks.title: Require --run-id for route, work, and validate
acceptance_criteria: Supervisor commands that operate on an existing run fail without an explicit --run-id argument.
work packages and routing still function correctly when --run-id is provided.
Init remains responsible only for creating a new run and printing its run id.
done_definition: The CLI cannot accidentally operate on an implicit repo-global run.

epics.title: Align docs and agent instructions with explicit run targeting

tasks.title: Update workflow documentation and supervisor instructions
acceptance_criteria: Docs show --run-id examples for all existing-run supervisor commands.
Agent instructions stop referring to an active-run pointer.
Repository operating rules state that existing-run commands must be explicit.
done_definition: Users can follow the docs without relying on hidden state.

assumptions: It is acceptable for users or tools to capture the run id from ai:init output and pass it explicitly afterwards.
The existing per-run artifact layout under .ai/runs is sufficient and does not need to change for this improvement.
risks: Removing implicit run selection makes commands slightly more verbose, so documentation clarity matters more.
Any local helper scripts or habits that depended on current-run.json will break until updated.
